articles.title: Status, Interpretation, and Constitutional Purpose
articles.summary: This Constitution establishes a fictional democratic constitutional commonwealth organized around distributed executive authority, generational representation, nonpartisan public elections, measurable administration, protected civil liberty, local productive capacity, technological advancement, material circulation, infrastructure resilience, and the preservation of society during catastrophic disruption.
excerpt: Section 1 — Nature of This Instrument

This Constitution establishes a fictional democratic constitutional commonwealth organized around distributed executive authority, generational representation, nonpartisan public elections, measurable administration, protected civil liberty, local productive capacity, technological advancement, material circulation, infrastructure resilience, and the preservation of society during catastrophic disruption.
Section 2 — Controlling Principles

Sovereignty originates in the people and is delegated only through this Constitution. No generation, official, institution, corporation, donor, military body, algorithm, or emergency authority may possess complete control of the Commonwealth. Public power must be lawful, temporary where appropriate, measurable, reviewable, and subject to meaningful appeal. Where provisions conflict, fundamental rights, due process, equal protection, and the least restrictive effective means shall control unless this Constitution expressly provides otherwise. Later and more specific provisions supersede earlier and more general provisions to the extent of direct conflict.

articles.title: Preamble and Foundational Order
articles.summary: We, the people of the Commonwealth, recognizing that liberty is endangered by concentrated power, that generations possess different experiences but equal dignity, that government promises must be measured against actual results, and that civilization must be prepared both to advance and to endure, establish this Constitution.
excerpt: We, the people of the Commonwealth, recognizing that liberty is endangered by concentrated power, that generations possess different experiences but equal dignity, that government promises must be measured against actual results, and that civilization must be prepared both to advance and to endure, establish this Constitution.

We establish a republic in which no generation governs alone; no public action escapes record and review; no private wealth purchases public authority; no technology advances without regard for human welfare; no useful material is treated as disposable by default; no essential system is permitted to remain needlessly fragile; and no emergency is permitted to destroy the constitutional order it claims to preserve.

The purposes of this Commonwealth are liberty, justice, public safety, equal protection, productive prosperity, local resilience, scientific advancement, human dignity, intergenerational fairness, and the continuous capacity to restore and improve civil society.

articles.title: Fundamental Rights and Liberties
articles.summary: Government shall establish no official religion and shall neither favor nor burden religious belief or nonbelief. No religious test shall be imposed for public office, public employment, voting, education, public benefit, or civic participation.
sections: Section — Freedom of Religion and Conscience
Section — Freedom of Speech and Expression

excerpt: Section — Freedom of Religion and Conscience

Government shall establish no official religion and shall neither favor nor burden religious belief or nonbelief. No religious test shall be imposed for public office, public employment, voting, education, public benefit, or civic participation.
Section — Freedom of Speech and Expression

Every person may speak, publish, create, criticize, receive lawful information, and express political, scientific, artistic, academic, religious, and personal views. Law may address direct threats, fraud, criminal solicitation, defamation, unlawful harassment, exploitation, or other clearly defined harmful conduct, but shall not punish lawful criticism of government.
Section — Freedom of the Press

The press shall remain independent from government direction. Government shall not license political journalism, compel favorable coverage, or secretly finance media influence. Generally applicable laws governing fraud, privacy, defamation, and crime remain enforceable.

textBlocks.text: Section — Freedom of Religion and Conscience

Law may address direct threats, fraud, criminal solicitation, defamation, unlawful harassment, exploitation, or other clearly defined harmful conduct, but shall not punish lawful criticism of government.
Section — Freedom of the Press

The press shall remain independent from government direction. Government shall not license political journalism, compel favorable coverage, or secretly finance media influence. Generally applicable laws governing fraud, privacy, defamation, and crime remain enforceable.
Section — Assembly, Association, and Petition

People may assemble peacefully, organize, associate, protest, demonstrate, form voluntary civic bodies, and petition government. No person may be compelled to join a political, religious, labor, professional, or civic organization.
Section — Right to Keep and Bear Arms

The people possess the right to lawfully keep and bear arms for defense and other lawful purposes. Regulation may address violent offenders, persons judicially determined to pose an immediate danger, commercial sale, safe storage, transport, manufacture, and use, but may not effectively abolish the protected right.
Section — Security of Person, Home, Property, Communications, and Data

People shall be secure against unreasonable search, seizure, surveillance, interception, collection, and examination. Warrants require probable cause, particularity, and issuance by an independent judicial officer. Digital records, biometric data, location information, communications, remotely stored files, and personal databases receive constitutional protection.
Section — Due Process and Criminal Procedure

No person shall be deprived of life, liberty, property, legal status, bodily autonomy, employment rights, or public benefits without due process. The accused is entitled to notice, counsel, a speedy and public trial, an impartial judge and jury, confrontation, compulsory process, silence, presumption of innocence, and proof beyond a reasonable doubt.
Section — Jury Trial
The right to jury trial shall be preserved in serious criminal matters and in substantial civil disputes as provided by law.
Section — Bail, Fines, and Punishment

Excessive bail and fines are prohibited. Cruel, degrading, or unusual punishment is prohibited. Punishment must remain proportionate and may not be designed primarily to inflict suffering or public spectacle.
Section — Equal Protection

All persons are equal before the law. Government shall not deny equal protection because of age, race, color, ethnicity, national origin, sex, disability, religion, belief, family status, economic class, or other protected status.
Section — Voting and Political Participation

Every citizen eighteen years of age or older possesses the right to vote. No poll tax, donation, party membership, property qualification, educational test, or financial contribution may be required to vote or seek office.
Section — Privacy and Bodily Autonomy

Every person possesses privacy in personal, familial, medical, reproductive, relational, and bodily matters. Restrictions must serve a compelling public purpose and use the least restrictive effective means.
Section — Technological Autonomy

Every person has the right to notice and meaningful human review when automated systems materially influence employment, education, credit, housing, medical treatment, benefits, legal status, or other consequential opportunities.
Section — Rights Retained by the People

The enumeration of rights shall not deny other rights retained by the people. Powers not delegated to the Commonwealth and not prohibited to local government remain with the people or their local institutions.

articles.title: The Generational Cohorts
articles.summary: For executive representation, every citizen belongs to exactly one age cohort according to age on the official election qualification date. Cohort membership affects executive voting and constitutionally authorized representative structures only; it shall not limit equal civil rights.
excerpt: For executive representation, every citizen belongs to exactly one age cohort according to age on the official election qualification date. Cohort membership affects executive voting and constitutionally authorized representative structures only; it shall not limit equal civil rights.

Cohort Ages Representative Perspective I 18–27 Education, technological adaptation, entry into work, housing access, and future generations II 28–38 Families, workforce mobility, entrepreneurship, childcare, and household formation III 39–49 Production, infrastructure, industrial coordination, and institutional management IV 50–59 National resilience, foreign affairs, defense, healthcare systems, and long-term strategy V 60 and older Retirement, fiscal continuity, institutional memory, and intergenerational stewardship
Transition Between Cohorts

A public official entering a new cohort acquires no automatic right to a new office. The official may compete in the next eligible election, and the complete public performance record shall remain permanently attached to the candidacy.

articles.title: The Generational Executive Council
articles.summary: Executive authority is vested collectively in five Cohort Executives, one elected by each cohort. Each Executive possesses one equal vote. No Executive may exercise unilateral national authority except where this Constitution expressly permits immediate temporary action.
excerpt: Collective Executive Authority

Executive authority is vested collectively in five Cohort Executives, one elected by each cohort. Each Executive possesses one equal vote. No Executive may exercise unilateral national authority except where this Constitution expressly permits immediate temporary action.

Decision Required Approval Ordinary executive action Three of five Executives Major national program, borrowing, extended emergency, or foreign military deployment Four of five Executives Offensive war Unanimous Executive Council and two-thirds of the Assembly Immediate defensive action Four Executives, subject to legislative review and automatic expiration
Terms, Stability, and Recall

Each Executive serves a five-year term, and one cohort election occurs each year. The first thirty months are a protected governing period during which removal may occur only for crime, corruption, incapacity, treason, grave ethical misconduct, constitutional violation, or deliberate falsification of government information. After thirty months, sustained performance failure may trigger a public confidence referendum under Article IX. No person may serve more than two consecutive terms in the same cohort or more than twenty years total as a Cohort Executive.

articles.title: The Assembly of State Delegations
articles.summary: Each state elects a single State Delegation composed of three delegates elected from separate legislative age bands. The delegation acts as one constitutional unit and casts one indivisible state vote.
excerpt: Tripartite State Representation

Each state elects a single State Delegation composed of three delegates elected from separate legislative age bands. The delegation acts as one constitutional unit and casts one indivisible state vote.
Delegate Age Band Emerging Delegate 18–34 Established Delegate 35–54 Senior Delegate 55 and older
Presence, Deliberation, and Unanimity

No substantive governmental deliberation, negotiation, testimony, amendment, commitment, recommendation, or vote may occur unless all three delegates are simultaneously present and the meeting is entered into the official record. Administrative scheduling and non-substantive staff coordination are permitted.
Every State Delegation decision requires unanimity. A delegation may record only Approval, Rejection, Abstention, or Unable to Reach Agreement. No divided or proportional vote is recognized.

textBlocks.text: Tripartite State Representation

Each state elects a single State Delegation composed of three delegates elected from separate legislative age bands. The delegation acts as one constitutional unit and casts one indivisible state vote.
Presence, Deliberation, and Unanimity

No substantive governmental deliberation, negotiation, testimony, amendment, commitment, recommendation, or vote may occur unless all three delegates are simultaneously present and the meeting is entered into the official record. Administrative scheduling and non-substantive staff coordination are permitted.
Every State Delegation decision requires unanimity. A delegation may record only Approval, Rejection, Abstention, or Unable to Reach Agreement. No divided or proportional vote is recognized.
Deadlock Procedure and Snap Election

items: Following an initial failed vote, the delegation observes a seventy-two-hour cooling period.
A second formal session shall occur, followed by a concise public deadlock statement from each delegate if unanimity remains impossible.
A mediated session shall occur within seven days.

Three major unresolved deadlocks within twelve months, failure to perform constitutional duties, joint request by two delegates, a lawful recall petition, or a Constitutional Court finding of deliberate obstruction may trigger a snap election.
A snap election contests all three seats. No single delegate may be removed merely because the other two disagree.
No state may conduct more than one delegation snap election in twelve months.

textBlocks.text: Continued deadlock thereafter results in abstention until the next regular election.
Double-Majority Legislative Passage

Ordinary legislation requires approval by a majority of all state delegations and by delegations representing a majority of the national population. Taxation, borrowing, war, constitutional amendment, emergency authority, and removal proceedings require higher thresholds established by this Constitution.
Restriction on Executive Service

A person elected to a State Delegation may not serve as a Cohort Executive until reaching Cohort V and completing a two-year separation period after legislative service. Legislative office creates no executive preference or automatic qualification.

articles.title: Nonpartisan Elections and the Prohibition of Political Finance
articles.summary: Political parties shall possess no official status in elections. Ballots shall not display party names, party symbols, donor classifications, commercial endorsements, organizational endorsements, or religious affiliation. Candidate order shall be randomized.
excerpt: Nonpartisan Ballots

Political parties shall possess no official status in elections. Ballots shall not display party names, party symbols, donor classifications, commercial endorsements, organizational endorsements, or religious affiliation. Candidate order shall be randomized.
Candidate Qualification

Citizenship and residency Cohort or legislative age eligibility Verified public petition signatures Public ethics screening Financial and conflict-of-interest disclosure Participation in official public debates and standardized candidate examinations
Absolute Prohibition of Political Donations

No person or organization may provide money, goods, services, discounts, transportation, advertising, data, staffing, gifts, loans, sponsorship, or anything of material value to a candidate, official, campaign, referendum effort, ballot action, legislative action, regulatory action, judicial selection, public program, procurement decision, or government agency. Officials and candidates may not solicit or accept such value.

items: Participation in official public debates and standardized candidate examinations

textBlocks.text: Absolute Prohibition of Political Donations

No person or organization may provide money, goods, services, discounts, transportation, advertising, data, staffing, gifts, loans, sponsorship, or anything of material value to a candidate, official, campaign, referendum effort, ballot action, legislative action, regulatory action, judicial selection, public program, procurement decision, or government agency. Officials and candidates may not solicit or accept such value.

Unpaid personal speech, journalism, criticism, endorsement, academic analysis, and ordinary civic discussion remain protected. Paid private promotion or opposition of a specific candidate during an official election period is prohibited.
Public Election Fund

Qualified candidates receive equal public access to communication, debates, standardized profiles, travel allowances, administrative support, and spending authority. Personal wealth may not supplement public campaign resources. Unused funds return to the Treasury and may not be transferred.

articles.title: The Judiciary and Constitutional Balance
articles.summary: The highest court shall contain fifteen members selected to provide professional, generational, regional, and intellectual diversity.
excerpt: Judicial Structure
State and Local Courts Regional Appellate Courts The National High Court The Constitutional Supreme Court The National Court for Grave Crimes
Constitutional Supreme Court
The highest court shall contain fifteen members selected to provide professional, generational, regional, and intellectual diversity.

Category Seats Qualification Senior judges 4 At least fifteen years of judicial experience Practicing lawyers 3 At least twelve years of legal practice Legal or constitutional scholars 3 Recognized expertise in law, history, government, or civil rights Nonlegal scholars 2 Ethics, economics, science, public administration, sociology, or related fields Advanced law students 2 Final-year students selected through a national process Civic member 1 Demonstrated public-service or civil-liberties experience

Student members participate fully in hearings, questioning, deliberation, and written opinions. They cast one joint vote only when both agree. Student terms are two years; other members serve staggered twelve-year nonrenewable terms.

textBlocks.text: Student members participate fully in hearings, questioning, deliberation, and written opinions. They cast one joint vote only when both agree. Student terms are two years; other members serve staggered twelve-year nonrenewable terms.
Appointment and Independence

Appointments are distributed among the Assembly, Executive Council, independent legal institutions, universities and scholarly bodies, and a National Civic Jury. No professional category may hold a majority. Judicial officers may be removed only for crime, corruption, incapacity, grave misconduct, deliberate constitutional violation, or persistent refusal to perform judicial duties.
National Court for Grave Crimes

The National Court for Grave Crimes exercises jurisdiction over aggravated murder, serial or mass killing, terrorism resulting in death, genocide, crimes against humanity, aggravated sexual violence, organized child exploitation, torture, severe human trafficking, and other nationally grave offenses designated by supermajority law.

The Court exists to establish reliable factual records, protect victims, impose lawful and proportionate punishment, preserve national consistency, and set authoritative precedent. It shall not operate for humiliation, spectacle, vengeance, propaganda, or the dehumanization of defendants.

Trials are heard by three professional judges and twelve jurors, supported by nonvoting victim-rights, defense-rights, and scientific evidence officers. Conviction requires a unanimous jury and certification by at least two judges that the proceeding complied with constitutional requirements. Automatic appeal, independent defense funding, disclosure of exculpatory evidence, recorded interrogations, scientific review, and post-conviction testing are required.
Public Finding of National Condemnation

After final conviction and exhaustion of direct appeal, the Court may publish a factual Public Finding of National Condemnation explaining the proven conduct, harm, legal violations, sentence, and prevention lessons. The finding may not encourage extrajudicial harm or theatrical humiliation.

articles.title: National Action Records
articles.summary: Every significant law, executive order, regulation, contract, infrastructure project, grant, tax change, emergency action, military action, public-benefit program, agency reorganization, continuation, renewal, or action materially affecting constitutional rights must possess a National Action Record before taking effect, except for immediate defensive emergencies. Emergency records must be published within seventy-two hours.
excerpt: Universal Record Requirement

Every significant law, executive order, regulation, contract, infrastructure project, grant, tax change, emergency action, military action, public-benefit program, agency reorganization, continuation, renewal, or action materially affecting constitutional rights must possess a National Action Record before taking effect, except for immediate defensive emergencies. Emergency records must be published within seventy-two hours.
Plain Language and Length

articles.title: The Office of National Performance and Accountability
articles.summary: The Office of National Performance and Accountability, known as ONPA, independently measures whether public actions produce promised outcomes at reasonable cost and time without imposing unacceptable burdens upon other cohorts, institutions, communities, or future generations. ONPA may audit, inspect, model, investigate, publish, and trigger review, but may not enact policy, command agencies, prosecute crimes, or remove elected officials.
excerpt: Mission

The Office of National Performance and Accountability, known as ONPA, independently measures whether public actions produce promised outcomes at reasonable cost and time without imposing unacceptable burdens upon other cohorts, institutions, communities, or future generations. ONPA may audit, inspect, model, investigate, publish, and trigger review, but may not enact policy, command agencies, prosecute crimes, or remove elected officials.
Cohort Performance Index

Measure Weight Policy effectiveness 25% Implementation efficiency 20% Fiscal responsibility 15% Delivery reliability 15% Intergenerational impact 10% Institutional health 10% Transparency and data integrity 5%

ONPA shall distinguish raw outcomes, expected outcomes under observed conditions, counterfactual outcomes without the policy, and outcomes reasonably attributable to official action. Every score must disclose methodology, data, assumptions, confidence ranges, known limits, and a right of response.
Governance

Governance

ONPA is governed by a fifteen-member National Performance Board consisting of five statistical experts, five systems or economic experts, and five public auditors. Members serve staggered fifteen-year nonrenewable terms. Universities and scientific institutions select five, professional organizations select five, and randomly selected Citizen Assemblies select five. Removal requires judicial findings of misconduct, corruption, incapacity, or deliberate manipulation of official statistics.

rows: Below 40 for twelve months after protected period

articles.title: Expiration, Review, and Closure of Public Actions
articles.summary: Every government program, regulation, authority, appropriation, and administrative action expires. Permanent programs are prohibited except where expressly created by this Constitution. Indefinite, continuing, automatic, or equivalent language shall not evade expiration.
excerpt: Presumption of Expiration

Every government program, regulation, authority, appropriation, and administrative action expires. Permanent programs are prohibited except where expressly created by this Constitution. Indefinite, continuing, automatic, or equivalent language shall not evade expiration.

Action Type Maximum Initial Duration Emergency authority 30 days Temporary regulation 1 year Pilot program 2 years Standard program 4 years Major infrastructure program 8 years Defense procurement 6 years Tax provision 5 years Public-benefit program 5 years Agency mandate 6 years
Mandatory Review Sequence

Startup review within ninety days Early implementation review within twelve months Midpoint review Pre-expiration review at least one hundred eighty days before expiration Closeout review within one hundred eighty days after termination
Findings

textBlocks.text: Presumption of Expiration

Every government program, regulation, authority, appropriation, and administrative action expires. Permanent programs are prohibited except where expressly created by this Constitution. Indefinite, continuing, automatic, or equivalent language shall not evade expiration.
Mandatory Review Sequence

items: Early implementation review within twelve months
Pre-expiration review at least one hundred eighty days before expiration
Closeout review within one hundred eighty days after termination

textBlocks.text: Findings

Formal reviews shall classify an action as Effective, Effective with Modifications, Inconclusive, Inefficient, Ineffective, Harmful, or Obsolete. Renewal requires a new public record demonstrating continued need, measurable effectiveness, reasonable efficiency, constitutional compliance, acceptable cohort distribution, updated costs, alternatives considered, a new expiration date, and named responsible officials.
Failure to complete renewal before expiration terminates the action. An expired program may not continue under a new name without being treated as a new action.

articles.title: The National Revenue Service and Public Finance
articles.summary: All national government income shall be collected through the National Revenue Service, which replaces the former federal income-tax administration system. Government agencies may not independently solicit or accept private donations, sponsorships, naming rights, conditional grants, or privately earmarked contributions.
excerpt: Exclusive Revenue Authority

All national government income shall be collected through the National Revenue Service, which replaces the former federal income-tax administration system. Government agencies may not independently solicit or accept private donations, sponsorships, naming rights, conditional grants, or privately earmarked contributions.
Administration

Automatic calculation and completed preliminary returns where possible No annual filing requirement for fully documented persons with zero liability Individual rather than household taxation No rate difference based on marriage, dependents, or relationship status Secure personal tax ledgers and plain-language explanations Independent appeals and judicial review Audit based on documented grounds Protection of taxpayer information from commercial or unauthorized government use
Individual Income Tax
The income tax is marginal: only the portion within each bracket is taxed at that bracket rate.

rows: Exempt on purchases of $15,000 or less; 3.5% on the full price of a purchase above $15,000
Exempt on purchases of $50,000 or less; 5% on the full price of a purchase above $50,000

textBlocks.text: Basic food, prescribed medicine, essential medical devices, primary residential rent, basic utilities, public transportation, and educational materials may be exempt by law through a reviewed National Action Record.
Protected Human Support Trust

Every dollar of individual annual income above ten million dollars is deposited into a protected trust supporting persons unable to support themselves because of age, permanent disability, serious medical condition, cognitive impairment, catastrophic illness or injury, or other medically verified incapacity. Funds may support income, housing, medical care, long-term care, assisted living, home health services, mobility, caregivers, transportation, nutrition, and accessibility. They may not fund military operations, general administration, corporate subsidies, elections, or unrelated debt.
Economic Substance

Income includes realized wages, salary, bonuses, contracts, business distributions, dividends, interest, realized capital gains, royalties, licensing, trust distributions, stock compensation, digital assets, foreign income, property exchanged for services, and other realized economic benefit. Artificial entities, sham loans, controlled corporations, offshore arrangements, and related-party transactions may not conceal income. Reclassification according to economic substance remains appealable.

articles.title: The Local Productive Economy, Employment Registry, and Consumer Commerce
articles.summary: Economic activity should occur at the smallest geographic and organizational level reasonably capable of providing the relevant good or service efficiently, safely, and competitively. Government shall encourage local ownership, local employment, independent enterprise, regional production, and distributed productive capacity without prohibiting lawful interstate or international commerce.
sections: Durable Product Standard and Right to Repair
excerpt: Economic Subsidiarity

Economic activity should occur at the smallest geographic and organizational level reasonably capable of providing the relevant good or service efficiently, safely, and competitively. Government shall encourage local ownership, local employment, independent enterprise, regional production, and distributed productive capacity without prohibiting lawful interstate or international commerce.
Community Economic Registries

Every municipality and state shall maintain an Economic Registry recording aggregate population, legitimate available jobs, filled jobs, occupations, compensation ranges, required skills, working hours, workforce shortages, housing availability, students, retirees, caregivers, persons seeking work, persons medically limited or unable to work, and public-support needs. A national system aggregates local data into a National Employment Exchange.

No public registry shall disclose an individual’s medical condition, disability, income, benefits, employment status, or other private information. Economic inactivity alone is not a crime. Persistent unexplained nonparticipation may trigger an offer of assessment, job matching, training, education, transportation, housing help, rehabilitation, treatment, or public service. No person may be compelled to accept private employment.
Employment Exchange Standards

A national system aggregates local data into a National Employment Exchange.

No public registry shall disclose an individual’s medical condition, disability, income, benefits, employment status, or other private information. Economic inactivity alone is not a crime. Persistent unexplained nonparticipation may trigger an offer of assessment, job matching, training, education, transportation, housing help, rehabilitation, treatment, or public service. No person may be compelled to accept private employment.
Employment Exchange Standards

items: Every legitimate vacancy must be registered and removed promptly when filled or withdrawn.
Compensation ranges, location, principal duties, hours, and material conditions are mandatory.
Legally necessary qualifications must be distinguished from employer preferences.
Knowingly publishing nonexistent jobs is prohibited.
Matching shall prioritize local opportunities before regional, state, and national opportunities when practical.

textBlocks.text: Durable Product Standard and Right to Repair

Every physical product must permit reasonable maintenance, repair, reuse, resale, continued operation, disassembly, component replacement, and data portability consistent with legitimate safety, security, performance, and environmental requirements. Manufacturers shall provide reasonable parts, diagnostics, repair documentation, software support, battery replacement where practical, and safe third-party repair.
Deliberate Commercial Obsolescence

No organization may knowingly design a product for premature failure, artificial incompatibility, unnecessary irreparability, deliberate degradation, or forced replacement where the design is not reasonably required for safety, security, substantial innovation, environmental protection, or technological performance. Proven violations may result in restitution, mandatory repair, fines, market restrictions, corporate probation, blacklisting, executive compensation clawbacks, industry bans, and judicial dissolution for persistent systemic misconduct.
Employee Protection During Dissolution

In corporate liquidation, priority shall be given to public safety and environmental obligations, victim compensation, unpaid wages, employee benefits, pensions, severance, transition income, small-business creditors, other creditors, and only then shareholders. A defined share of remaining assets shall be distributed equally among qualifying employees who did not knowingly participate in misconduct. Responsible executives are excluded. Personal assets may be seized only by lawful judgment and only to the extent of criminal proceeds, fraudulent transfers, unlawful compensation, restitution, or proportionate penalties.

articles.title: Subscription Continuity and Tier Non-Regression
articles.summary: These foundational subscription protections remain operative and are broadened, restated, and supplemented by Article XXXVI concerning the entire consumer-services and digital-property economy. Where the two Articles overlap, Article XXXVI controls.
excerpt: These foundational subscription protections remain operative and are broadened, restated, and supplemented by Article XXXVI concerning the entire consumer-services and digital-property economy. Where the two Articles overlap, Article XXXVI controls.
Price Continuity

A provider may not increase the recurring price of an existing subscription tier while continuing to provide a materially equivalent service. Existing subscribers retain the agreed price unless they voluntarily change plans, terminate service, or the provider lawfully discontinues the product under this Article.
Higher Tiers

A higher-priced tier may be created only when it offers substantial new products, services, functionality, content categories, capacity, or measurable consumer benefit. Advertisement removal, restoration of removed features, ordinary technical quality, basic security, bug fixes, customer service, or artificially imposed restrictions do not independently constitute material additional value.
Non-Regression Once Subscribed

textBlocks.text: These foundational subscription protections remain operative and are broadened, restated, and supplemented by Article XXXVI concerning the entire consumer-services and digital-property economy. Where the two Articles overlap, Article XXXVI controls.
Price Continuity

A provider may not increase the recurring price of an existing subscription tier while continuing to provide a materially equivalent service. Existing subscribers retain the agreed price unless they voluntarily change plans, terminate service, or the provider lawfully discontinues the product under this Article.
Higher Tiers

A higher-priced tier may be created only when it offers substantial new products, services, functionality, content categories, capacity, or measurable consumer benefit. Advertisement removal, restoration of removed features, ordinary technical quality, basic security, bug fixes, customer service, or artificially imposed restrictions do not independently constitute material additional value.
Non-Regression Once Subscribed

Once at least one person subscribes to a defined tier, the provider may not materially reduce, degrade, restrict, or remove the features, service quality, access conditions, content categories, advertising conditions, storage, performance, device access, or principal benefits included when that subscriber enrolled.

items: Advertisements may not be added to a tier that was advertisement-free when the subscriber enrolled.
Included features may not be removed and resold through a higher tier or add-on.
Storage, usage allowances, device access, quality, or performance may not be reduced to manufacture demand for a more expensive tier.
A tier may not be renamed, restructured, nominally discontinued, or transferred to a successor entity to evade obligations.
Silence, continued use, failure to respond, or acceptance of general updated terms does not constitute consent to material regression.

textBlocks.text: Discontinuation

A subscribed tier may be discontinued only when continued operation becomes technically impossible, unlawful, or economically unsustainable for reasons not deliberately created by the provider. The provider must give twelve months’ notice, submit supporting evidence, offer a materially equivalent replacement at no greater recurring price, permit penalty-free cancellation, support data export, and refund unavailable prepaid service. Declining expected profit or desire for greater revenue is insufficient.
Cancellation

Cancellation must be no more burdensome than enrollment and available through the same medium. Mandatory telephone calls, hidden procedures, retention interrogation, or unreasonable delay are prohibited. Consumers receive confirmation, trial-conversion notice, periodic total-payment disclosure, and reasonable data export.

articles.title: Technological Advancement and Human Safety
articles.summary: The National Agency for Technological Advancement and Human Safety shall accelerate technology that measurably improves scientific capability, prosperity, productivity, human welfare, medicine, infrastructure, or national resilience while identifying and reducing physical, psychological, developmental, economic, environmental, and societal harms. Neither advancement nor safety automatically dominates; the objective is maximum long-term societal benefit consistent with liberty.
excerpt: Agency and Dual Mandate

The National Agency for Technological Advancement and Human Safety shall accelerate technology that measurably improves scientific capability, prosperity, productivity, human welfare, medicine, infrastructure, or national resilience while identifying and reducing physical, psychological, developmental, economic, environmental, and societal harms. Neither advancement nor safety automatically dominates; the objective is maximum long-term societal benefit consistent with liberty.
Freedom of Innovation

No technology may be prohibited merely because it is new, disruptive, economically transformative, politically controversial, or inconsistent with existing institutions. Restrictions require specific evidence of substantial harm or a substantial probability of harm and must use the least restrictive effective means.
National Talent Service and Challenges

A voluntary National Talent Service shall identify scientists, engineers, physicians, mathematicians, economists, psychologists, skilled tradespeople, programmers, inventors, researchers, entrepreneurs, safety specialists, statisticians, ethicists, and exceptional students through demonstrated ability rather than credentials alone. National Challenges shall define measurable public problems, permit competing solutions, fund milestones, test prototypes, scale successful results, and terminate failed programs.

Human Impact Office

An independent Office of Human Impact shall investigate physical injury, disease, neurological effects, sleep, workplace safety, addiction, anxiety, depression, attention degradation, compulsive behavior, isolation, manipulation, child development, privacy, autonomy, labor displacement, and societal stability. Officials promoting a technology may not suppress unfavorable findings.
Children’s Technology Protection

Technologies knowingly designed for, marketed to, or substantially used by children are subject to heightened review of addictive design, compulsive notifications, infinite scrolling, targeted advertising, behavioral experimentation, data collection, algorithmic exposure, account deletion, and repeated recommendation of harmful material.
Government shall regulate exploitative mechanisms, not lawful ideas, beliefs, inquiry, or discussion.
Impact Classification and Advancement Zones

rows: Potential developmental, psychological, economic, or physical harm

textBlocks.text: Voluntary Technology Advancement Zones may permit accelerated testing with informed consent, temporary regulatory flexibility, anonymized outcome data, ONPA measurement, and full constitutional protection.
Technology National Action Records

Every major government technology program and significant technology regulation requires a ten-page Technology National Action Record addressing the problem, technology, societal benefit, economic effect, physical risk, psychological and developmental risk, rights implications, implementation, success and failure measures, and expiration. Major technology regulations expire within five years unless renewed on new evidence.
Public Technology and Capture Prevention

Technology developed entirely with public resources presumptively belongs to the public, subject to narrow national-security, privacy, safety, or strategic restrictions. Agency officials may not hold substantial regulated-company interests, accept gifts or industry-funded travel, or accept employment commitments. Senior officials face a five-year restriction on employment by organizations they directly regulated. Substantive meetings are public records.

Agency officials may not hold substantial regulated-company interests, accept gifts or industry-funded travel, or accept employment commitments. Senior officials face a five-year restriction on employment by organizations they directly regulated. Substantive meetings are public records.

articles.title: Material Circulation, Resource Recovery, and the Elimination of Landfills
articles.summary: No material product may enter commerce without an approved pathway for continued use, maintenance, repair, reuse, redistribution, disassembly, component recovery, material recovery, recycling, biological processing, industrial conversion, or secure temporary containment. Disposal is treated as a failure of design, production, collection, or administration.
sections: Central Materials Recovery and Recycling Authority
Product Passports and Design for Recovery
excerpt: National Principle

Disposal is treated as a failure of design, production, collection, or administration.
Central Materials Recovery and Recycling Authority

The Central Materials Recovery and Recycling Authority establishes national standards for material identity, composition reporting, disassembly, collection, transport, sorting, cleaning, reuse, remanufacturing, recycling, biological processing, hazardous recovery, unresolved containment, and redistribution. It maintains the National Material Circulation Database and the National Material Bucket System.
Universal Coverage and Producer Responsibility

Every physical product, component, package, consumable, industrial material, construction material, commercial material, and tangible good is covered regardless of size, weight, price, material, lifespan, quantity, origin, or method of sale. Large producers maintain internal Material Recovery Divisions; smaller producers may join certified cooperatives or shared systems. Responsibility cannot be transferred merely to evade obligations.

items: Complete material composition within reasonable tolerances
Residual material and current recovery limitations

textBlocks.text: National Material Bucket System

Equivalent materials used by different companies shall use common national categories. Collection infrastructure is organized around material compatibility rather than brand identity. Proprietary collection categories are prohibited where a national bucket reasonably applies.
Product Passports and Design for Recovery

Every material product shall possess a durable physical or machine-readable identity providing composition, repair, disassembly, hazards, bucket assignment, collection locations, and recovery pathways. Products shall avoid unnecessary permanent adhesives, inseparable mixtures, proprietary fasteners, destructive disassembly, unmarked materials, nonreplaceable consumables, and software restrictions preventing reuse.
Universal Return and Material Hierarchy

Every resident shall have reasonable access to municipal, retail, producer, mail, automated, scheduled, industrial, or certified private return. Management priority is continued use, repair, direct reuse, redistribution, refurbishment, component harvest, remanufacture, material recycling, biological processing, industrial conversion, energy recovery where no superior path exists, and only then secure temporary containment.
Landfill Prohibition

New landfills for ordinary municipal, commercial, or industrial waste are prohibited after the transition established by law. Existing landfills shall cease receiving ordinary waste within fifteen years. Progressive diversion, increasing fees, infrastructure construction, closure planning, monitoring, and landfill mining shall occur during transition. Permanent untracked burial of ordinary material waste is prohibited.
Unresolved Material Repositories

Materials for which no safe and technically feasible recovery, destruction, conversion, or reuse exists may enter engineered repositories. Such materials remain inventoried, separated, accessible, monitored, periodically reviewed, and assigned to responsible owners or public custodians. These repositories are not landfills and do not extinguish producer obligations.

articles.title: Homeland Infrastructure Stability and Civilization Continuity
articles.summary: The Commonwealth, states, municipalities, public authorities, and critical operators shall maintain permanent capability to preserve essential human needs and minimum civil order during severe disruption. Critical systems shall be designed for survivability, redundancy, decentralization, repairability, reserve capacity, manual fallback, rapid restoration, and degraded operation, not merely lowest cost or ordinary efficiency.
sections: National Continuity and Infrastructure Stability Authority
excerpt: National Duty

The Commonwealth, states, municipalities, public authorities, and critical operators shall maintain permanent capability to preserve essential human needs and minimum civil order during severe disruption. Critical systems shall be designed for survivability, redundancy, decentralization, repairability, reserve capacity, manual fallback, rapid restoration, and degraded operation, not merely lowest cost or ordinary efficiency.
Essential Continuity Systems

Essential systems include drinking water, water treatment, sewage, wastewater, electricity, fuel and gas, internet and telecommunications, emergency communications, hospitals, emergency medical services, fire and rescue, essential medicines, food production and distribution, critical transportation, payment systems necessary for essential commerce, material recovery required for safety, and any system whose prolonged failure threatens life, health, civil order, or national survival.
National Continuity and Infrastructure Stability Authority

The Authority establishes minimum standards, maintains the National Infrastructure Dependency Map, coordinates reserves and exercises, inspects readiness, identifies vulnerabilities, and publishes Continuity Readiness Reports while leaving ordinary operation distributed among local, state, cooperative, private, and regional operators.

textBlocks.text: National Duty

The Commonwealth, states, municipalities, public authorities, and critical operators shall maintain permanent capability to preserve essential human needs and minimum civil order during severe disruption. Critical systems shall be designed for survivability, redundancy, decentralization, repairability, reserve capacity, manual fallback, rapid restoration, and degraded operation, not merely lowest cost or ordinary efficiency.

The Authority establishes minimum standards, maintains the National Infrastructure Dependency Map, coordinates reserves and exercises, inspects readiness, identifies vulnerabilities, and publishes Continuity Readiness Reports while leaving ordinary operation distributed among local, state, cooperative, private, and regional operators.
Geographic Continuity Cells

Every resident is geographically assigned for planning to a Continuity Cell composed of a municipality or practical regional group. Each Cell maintains population, water, food, power, sanitation, medical, communications, fuel, workforce, transportation, vulnerable-population, dependency, reserve, and restoration plans.

Cells shall maintain thirty days of minimum independent operation initially, ninety days within ten years, and later differentiated standards based on geography, climate, density, agricultural capacity, infrastructure risk, and strategic importance.
Sector Safeguards

items: Water: multiple sources, emergency wells, reservoirs, mobile purification, distributed storage, gravity capability, backup pumping, chemicals, testing, and spare components.
Sanitation: backup power, emergency pumping, bypass procedures, mobile systems, manual control, containment, chemicals, and priority repair.
Electricity: distributed generation, microgrids, segmentation, black-start, storage, spare transformers, mobile generation, local controls, multiple fuels, and priority restoration.
Fuel and gas: geographic reserves, sectional isolation, alternate suppliers, multiple transport methods, and priority allocation.
Communications: multiple backbones, emergency radio, satellite, mesh networks, local servers, public broadcast, offline information, and independent power.
Medical: surge capacity, mobile facilities, reserve personnel, medicine, blood, oxygen, sterilization, independent power, water, transport, and geographically distributed reserves.
Food: local and regional production maps, storage, refrigeration, transport, fuel, water, seed, fertilizer, feed, machinery parts, and rotating nutritional reserves.

textBlocks.text: Manual and Analog Fallback

No essential system may become impossible to operate solely because software, remote authentication, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, satellite navigation, or public internet fails. Local controls, manual overrides, offline documentation, printed procedures, physical access, local authentication, and non-cloud operating modes shall exist where technically reasonable.
Exercises, Scores, and Domestic Capability

Every Continuity Cell and essential system receives a public Infrastructure Stability Score measuring redundancy, reserve capacity, geographic distribution, repairability, domestic supply, cyber and physical security, workforce, manual fallback, restoration speed, autonomy duration, exercise performance, and unresolved dependencies. Realistic multi-system exercises are mandatory. The Nation shall preserve domestic, allied redundant, reserve, or rapid-manufacturing capability for every category necessary to survival and restoration.

articles.title: National Survival Mode and the Preservation of Civil Society
articles.summary: National Survival Mode is the final constitutional apparatus for conditions in which ordinary government, infrastructure, commerce, communications, healthcare, food distribution, public safety, or administration can no longer maintain minimum national survival. Recession, protest, political opposition, labor action, criticism, electoral instability, or ordinary crime do not independently justify activation.
sections: Preservation of Knowledge, Agriculture, and Industry
excerpt: Purpose and Activation

National Survival Mode is the final constitutional apparatus for conditions in which ordinary government, infrastructure, commerce, communications, healthcare, food distribution, public safety, or administration can no longer maintain minimum national survival. Recession, protest, political opposition, labor action, criticism, electoral instability, or ordinary crime do not independently justify activation.

Activation requires four of five Cohort Executives, two-thirds of State Delegations, emergency review by the Constitutional Supreme Court, and a Survival Declaration Record. If communication makes approval temporarily impossible, four Executives may activate the mode for no more than seven days.
Survival Levels
Level Condition 1 Severe regional failure 2 Multi-regional system failure 3 National essential-system failure 4 Fragmented national operations 5 Civilization preservation condition
Distributed Survival Governance

textBlocks.text: Purpose and Activation

National Survival Mode is the final constitutional apparatus for conditions in which ordinary government, infrastructure, commerce, communications, healthcare, food distribution, public safety, or administration can no longer maintain minimum national survival. Recession, protest, political opposition, labor action, criticism, electoral instability, or ordinary crime do not independently justify activation.

Activation requires four of five Cohort Executives, two-thirds of State Delegations, emergency review by the Constitutional Supreme Court, and a Survival Declaration Record. If communication makes approval temporarily impossible, four Executives may activate the mode for no more than seven days.
Survival Levels
Distributed Survival Governance

Authority remains distributed among the Executive Council, Assembly, Constitutional Supreme Court, Regional Survival Commands, Geographic Continuity Cells, Continuity Authority, ONPA, essential operators, and local emergency governments. No institution may consolidate all legislative, executive, military, judicial, communications, and resource-allocation power.
Minimum Civil Quality of Life

items: Personal dignity, meaningful work, culture, communication, and recreation where feasible

textBlocks.text: Survival Economy and Property

When currency or banking fails, government may activate vouchers, ration credits, local exchange certificates, emergency accounts, recorded labor credits, controlled allotments, temporary essential-goods price controls, anti-hoarding rules, and community production agreements. These mechanisms do not permanently replace lawful currency.

Private property remains protected. Temporary use is permitted only to preserve life, restore infrastructure, provide shelter, support medicine or food, or protect public safety; use must be documented and compensated where practicable. Political confiscation and permanent redistribution without ordinary process are prohibited.
Critical Labor and Vulnerable Populations

Temporary critical service may be required only where immediate danger to life or essential systems exists, matched to ability, limited in duration, compensated, and subject to medical, caregiving, hardship, and legal review. Children, elderly persons, disabled persons, medically dependent persons, pregnant persons, infants, institutionalized populations, homeless populations, and persons requiring ongoing medication receive specific protection.
Preservation of Knowledge, Agriculture, and Industry

Distributed repositories shall preserve the Constitution, laws, records, medical and engineering knowledge, agriculture, science, infrastructure maps, education, manufacturing instructions, critical software and source code, and restoration procedures in digital and durable physical forms. Machine tools, foundries, repair shops, water equipment, pharmaceuticals, agriculture, communications, transport repair, material recovery, essential chemicals, and computing controls receive preservation priority.
Duration, Review, and Return

An initial declaration lasts no more than thirty days. Renewal requires four Executives, two-thirds of Delegations, Court review, an updated record, and evidence that ordinary systems remain incapable. Renewal may not be automatic. Emergency institutions terminate when ordinary systems can again provide minimum services and constitutional administration. Records, property claims, detention, compulsory labor, elections, courts, spending, corruption, and rights restrictions must be reconciled and reviewed.
Absolute Prohibitions

Survival Mode may not cancel opposition, punish journalists, establish permanent censorship or military government, cancel elections indefinitely, alter cohort boundaries, permanently extend terms, eliminate judicial review, establish hereditary authority, impose collective punishment, arbitrarily remove citizenship, conduct involuntary experimentation, or create permanent emergency law without ordinary ratification.

articles.title: Public Budget Transparency, Lobbying, and Ethical Government
articles.summary: Every public expenditure must trace to a lawful revenue source, legislative appropriation, National Action Record, responsible agency, responsible official, stated purpose, expiration date, and review schedule. Budgets shall be published in human-readable, machine-readable, transaction-level, and simplified forms. Secret appropriations are prohibited; classified spending remains subject to independent audit and judicial review.
excerpt: Traceable Public Spending

Every public expenditure must trace to a lawful revenue source, legislative appropriation, National Action Record, responsible agency, responsible official, stated purpose, expiration date, and review schedule. Budgets shall be published in human-readable, machine-readable, transaction-level, and simplified forms. Secret appropriations are prohibited; classified spending remains subject to independent audit and judicial review.
Petition Without Purchased Access

People and organizations retain the right to petition government through documented public channels. Private paid access is prohibited. Substantive communications concerning legislation, regulation, procurement, taxation, appointments, contracts, enforcement, and National Action Records must be disclosed. Advocates may submit evidence and testimony but may not provide officials money, gifts, services, travel, employment promises, or other benefits.
Revolving-Door Restrictions

Officials may not accept employment from an organization substantially affected by actions they personally controlled for a period established by law. Agencies with specialized regulation may impose longer periods, including the five-year restriction governing senior technology officials.

articles.summary: Government shall evaluate how actions distribute costs, benefits, risks, debt, environmental consequences, infrastructure obligations, technological harms, material burdens, and institutional effects across generations. No cohort may permanently transfer unreasonable costs to another cohort or future generations without explicit disclosure, supermajority authorization where required, and independent review.

articles.title: States, Municipalities, and Distributed Administration
articles.summary: States and municipalities retain authority not delegated to the Commonwealth or prohibited by this Constitution. Local government shall administer community services, economic registries, Continuity Cells, public safety, infrastructure operations, material collection, local courts, and other lawful functions consistent with national rights and interoperability standards.

articles.title: Emergency Powers and Constitutional Continuity
articles.summary: Emergency authority must be specific, publicly justified, geographically and substantively limited, reviewable, and automatically expiring. Emergency declarations shall identify the condition, affected area, activated powers, rights effects, responsible officials, review dates, and termination conditions.

articles.title: National Defense and Civilian Control
articles.summary: Military authority remains subordinate to civilian constitutional government. Offensive war requires unanimous Executive Council approval and two-thirds of State Delegations. Defensive action may begin under the higher executive threshold established by Article IV but expires without legislative authorization.

articles.title: Public Office, Fiduciary Duty, and Personal Accountability
articles.summary: Every official is a public fiduciary and must act for the public benefit rather than personal, commercial, familial, organizational, or factional advantage. Financial interests, gifts, outside positions, professional relationships, and conflicts must be disclosed. Deliberate obstruction of lawful auditing, falsification of public data, concealment of material risk, bribery, profiteering, and theft of public or survival resources are constitutional offenses.

articles.title: Implementation, Measurement, and Constitutional Simulation
articles.summary: Before full implementation, major institutional mechanisms may be tested through transparent simulations, pilot programs, constitutional stress tests, and controlled regional trials. Simulation shall not substitute for democratic authorization or be represented as certainty.

articles.title: Domestic Human Security, Homelessness Elimination, and Suspension of Foreign Aid
articles.summary: The direct material obligations of the Commonwealth shall be fulfilled toward its own citizens before discretionary public resources are transferred abroad. This Article establishes a measurable domestic sufficiency standard, a permanent homelessness-elimination system, treatment and protective housing for citizens unable to care for themselves, and a constitutional suspension of foreign aid until domestic human security is independently certified.
excerpt: The direct material obligations of the Commonwealth shall be fulfilled toward its own citizens before discretionary public resources are transferred abroad. This Article establishes a measurable domestic sufficiency standard, a permanent homelessness-elimination system, treatment and protective housing for citizens unable to care for themselves, and a constitutional suspension of foreign aid until domestic human security is independently certified.
Section 1 — Domestic Priority

The Commonwealth shall first secure shelter, food, potable water, sanitation, essential medical care, personal safety, and necessary support for citizens who cannot safely support themselves. Foreign aid shall remain suspended until every state has achieved and maintained the Domestic Human Security Standard.
Section 2 — Foreign Aid Defined

Foreign aid includes grants, subsidized loans, military assistance transferred without full payment, foreign budget support, development assistance, debt forgiveness intended as assistance, public humanitarian transfers abroad, and material support furnished without equivalent compensation. Accounting form, intermediaries, or reclassification shall not defeat this definition.
Section 3 — Excluded International Activity

This Article establishes a measurable domestic sufficiency standard, a permanent homelessness-elimination system, treatment and protective housing for citizens unable to care for themselves, and a constitutional suspension of foreign aid until domestic human security is independently certified.
Section 1 — Domestic Priority

The Commonwealth shall first secure shelter, food, potable water, sanitation, essential medical care, personal safety, and necessary support for citizens who cannot safely support themselves. Foreign aid shall remain suspended until every state has achieved and maintained the Domestic Human Security Standard.
Section 2 — Foreign Aid Defined

Foreign aid includes grants, subsidized loans, military assistance transferred without full payment, foreign budget support, development assistance, debt forgiveness intended as assistance, public humanitarian transfers abroad, and material support furnished without equivalent compensation. Accounting form, intermediaries, or reclassification shall not defeat this definition.
Section 3 — Excluded International Activity

Ordinary commerce, payment for goods or services, diplomacy, intelligence and law-enforcement cooperation, evacuation of citizens, mutually beneficial research, treaty performance yielding direct national benefit, and measures necessary to prevent a direct threat to the Commonwealth are not foreign aid. These exceptions shall be narrowly interpreted and documented through National Action Records.
Section 4 — Domestic Human Security Standard

items: Functional emergency shelter sufficient for all citizens lacking safe housing, with no persistent waiting list.
A permanent supportive-housing supply sufficient for citizens requiring long-term assistance.
Safe Haven capacity for citizens unable to maintain independent living because of severe mental disorder, cognitive impairment, disability, medical fragility, or disabling substance-use disorder.
Reasonable access to food, water, sanitation, emergency medicine, mental-health treatment, substance-use treatment, and protection from violence or exploitation.
Independent verification that services are actually available, geographically accessible, and sustainably funded rather than merely authorized in law.

textBlocks.text: Section 5 — National Homelessness Elimination Program

A National Homelessness Elimination Program shall coordinate municipal, state, medical, housing, employment, and public-safety systems. It shall maintain emergency shelter, rapid rehousing, permanent supportive housing, medical respite housing, recovery housing, family housing, veteran housing, youth housing, elder housing, disability-accessible housing, and relocation for victims of domestic violence.
Section 6 — Individual Stability Assessment

A citizen experiencing persistent homelessness shall be offered a confidential assessment of shelter, medical, psychiatric, substance-use, cognitive, disability, employment, education, family, violence, transportation, identification, and independent-living needs. The assessment shall exist to match assistance, not to create a public stigma or permanent civic classification.
Section 7 — Functional Capacity and Participation

Support classifications may distinguish independent employability, employability with training or accommodations, temporary medical limitation, partial capacity, inability to work, inability to live independently, and need for supervised treatment. Citizens capable of productive participation may be required, as a condition of continuing nonemergency assistance, to participate reasonably in employment search, education, training, rehabilitation, public service, or treatment planning. Emergency food, water, shelter, and medically necessary care shall never be denied for nonparticipation.
Section 8 — Safe Haven Program

The National Safe Haven Program shall provide secure, clean, non-carceral housing and supervised care for persons unable to provide reliably for their own survival or safety. Facilities shall provide medical and psychiatric care, substance-use treatment, privacy, outdoor access, communication, recreation, meaningful activity, legal advocacy, regular reassessment, and a pathway to less restrictive living whenever possible.
Section 9 — Voluntary and Protective Placement

Placement shall ordinarily be voluntary. Nonconsensual protective placement requires clear evidence of functional incapacity, substantial risk of serious harm, and the absence of a safe less restrictive alternative. Emergency placement shall receive prompt judicial review; continued placement requires counsel, medical evidence, independent review, and recurring judicial reassessment.
Section 10 — Employment and Reintegration

Citizens capable of work shall receive access to registered employment, skills assessment, apprenticeships, public employment, transitional employment, transportation, identification recovery, childcare, work equipment, disability accommodation, and record-reentry assistance. Public support shall taper rather than terminate abruptly when employment begins.
Section 11 — Certification and Resumption

ONPA shall conduct an annual Domestic Human Security Review. Certification requires verification by ONPA, national health and continuity authorities, and the Constitutional Supreme Court. Certification lasts one year and merely removes the prohibition; it does not compel foreign aid. If homelessness, shelter capacity, Safe Haven access, treatment access, or support for medically incapable citizens materially fails, certification shall be withdrawn and new foreign commitments shall stop.
Section 12 — Private Charity

Private citizens and organizations may voluntarily assist foreign populations using private resources, subject to ordinary laws concerning fraud, sanctions, terrorism financing, and public safety. The government shall not compel, subsidize, match, or administer such assistance while foreign aid remains suspended.
Section 13 — Redirected Public Resources

Appropriations formerly assigned to foreign aid shall be redirected first to emergency shelter, permanent supportive housing, Safe Haven construction, mental-health and substance-use treatment, medical respite, disability support, employment reintegration, homelessness prevention, and affordable housing production. Every transfer shall remain traceable through a National Action Record.
Section 14 — Rights and Due Process

Homelessness, mental illness, substance use, disability, unemployment, or receipt of assistance shall not justify disenfranchisement, humiliation, public identification, permanent civic exclusion, or deprivation of liberty without individualized due process.

articles.title: Housing Access, Limited Ownership, Nonprofit Transfer, and Income-Based Occupancy
articles.summary: Housing shall serve first as a place of residence and human stability rather than an unrestricted vehicle for speculation. This Article establishes limits on residential accumulation, a guaranteed path to first ownership, controlled transfer values, nonprofit rental operation, mandatory income-based affordability, and conversion of unused commercial property when lowest-tier housing demand is unmet.
sections: Section 9 — Prohibition of Residential Resale Profit
Section 13 — Lawful Rent and Small-Unit Ceiling
Section 21 — Owner Opportunity and Compulsory Acquisition
excerpt: Housing shall serve first as a place of residence and human stability rather than an unrestricted vehicle for speculation. This Article establishes limits on residential accumulation, a guaranteed path to first ownership, controlled transfer values, nonprofit rental operation, mandatory income-based affordability, and conversion of unused commercial property when lowest-tier housing demand is unmet.
Section 1 — Residential Ownership Limits
